description: SQL safety regex (_FORBIDDEN, db.py:20) rejects any SELECT whose string-literal or LIKE-pattern contains a forbidden keyword as a substring (DROP/CREATE/EXEC/...). Common legitimate queries like WHERE organization_nm LIKE '%CREATE%' or WHERE manager_name LIKE '%Drop%' are blocked, so the assistant cannot answer questions about clients/managers whose names contain those substrings.
reproducer: from text2sql.db import assert_safe_select; assert_safe_select("SELECT * FROM client_product WHERE organization_nm LIKE '%CREATE%'") -> SQLSafetyError

description: assert_safe_select forbids any ';' anywhere except trailing, including semicolons inside Oracle string literals. Legitimate filters like manager_name = 'Иванов; И.В.' are rejected as 'multi-statement'.
reproducer: assert_safe_select("SELECT * FROM client_product WHERE manager_name = 'Иванов; И.В.'") -> SQLSafetyError(Multi-statement SQL не поддерживается)

description: _to_jsonable converts Decimal whose value equals its integral part to int and otherwise to float. For Oracle NUMBER(18,2) sums (CA_LCY_SUM, PNL_SUM, FX_VOLUME_RUB), large monetary aggregates lose precision: Decimal('9999999999999999999.99') becomes float 1e+19 (scientific notation, lost cents, lost 4+ significant digits). LLM then reports inaccurate rouble totals from this lossy JSON payload.
reproducer: from decimal import Decimal; from text2sql.db import _to_jsonable; print(_to_jsonable(Decimal('9999999999999999999.99')))  # -> 1e+19

description: When the LLM returns content with no tool_calls (common with Ollama/Qwen when tool-calling is flaky), the agent treats the raw chat text as the final answer and exits 0 — without ever hitting execute_sql or final_answer. The user gets a hallucinated answer with no real DB numbers, while the framework reports success.
reproducer: Code path agent.py:94-103: 'if not tool_calls' → return AgentRun(answer=msg.content). No guard requiring final_answer tool to have been used.

description: Neither OracleClient.execute_select nor the OpenAI client has a call/timeout. A heavy aggregation or wrong join in execute_sql will hang the agent indefinitely; an unresponsive Ollama hangs the whole process. No max wall-clock guard exists either.
reproducer: Code path: oracledb.connect(...) and cur.execute(sql) called with no call_timeout; OpenAI(base_url=...) built with no timeout in llm.py:12.

description: SQL examples and prompt teach the agent to use `MONTH_DT = (SELECT MAX(MONTH_DT) FROM client_product)` for 'последний месяц'. metadata.py explicitly warns the max MONTH_DT can be a mid-pipeline snapshot of the NOT-YET-CLOSED current month. The agent will silently mix incomplete current-month data with closed-month context (e.g. 'остатки за последний месяц'), returning misleadingly small numbers.
reproducer: metadata.py:26-43 vs sql_examples returning MAX(MONTH_DT) (metadata.py:419, 427, 431, 435, 459, 471).

description: validator.non_null_columns only fires when ALL rows of the column are NULL; a column where most values are NULL (e.g. 90%) passes silently. Likewise non_negative_columns fires on legitimately-negative PNL (loss) values, encouraging false warnings that may push the LLM to re-run a correct query or hedge the answer.
reproducer: from text2sql.validator import validate; print(validate({'columns':['x'],'rows':[[None],[None],[1]],'row_count':3}, {'non_null_columns':['x']})) -> ok=True

description: Row limit is hard-coded to 1000 with no pagination tool, and inspect_schema only knows one table. Listing-style questions ('перечисли всех клиентов', ~ thousands of rows) silently truncate; the LLM sees a `truncated:true` flag but has no path to follow up other than re-aggregating. The agent has no way to surface that the answer is partial unless it remembers to mention it.
reproducer: Code path: db.py:90 row_limit=1000; fetchmany(row_limit+1) then drop overflow; only one table in list_tables() (schema.py:51-53).

description: assert_safe_select rejects any SQL whose first non-whitespace tokens are an Oracle SQL comment (-- or /* */). Both common forms ('-- комментарий\nSELECT 1 FROM dual' and '/* hi */ SELECT 1') fail because the allowed-prefix regex isn't comment-aware. A model that prepends an explanation comment, or inline /* DROP */ inside a SELECT, has its valid SELECT rejected.
reproducer: assert_safe_select('-- комментарий\nSELECT 1 FROM dual') -> SQLSafetyError(Разрешены только SELECT / WITH).

description: ToolDispatcher.dispatch swallows EVERY exception, including oracledb connection errors (network down, wrong DSN, auth failure), and returns the message back to the LLM as a tool-result string. The LLM has no way to distinguish 'SQL is wrong, retry' from 'DB unreachable, retry pointless', so it loops up to max_iterations doing pointless retries, then emits a generic 'не удалось получить ответ за 10 шагов' with exit code 0.
reproducer: Code path: tools.py:162-166 wraps all handler exceptions in {'error': ...}; agent.py:152-163 returns code-0 success after max_iterations.

description: _inspect_schema falls back to 'client_product' when args.get('table') is empty/None instead of returning an error. If the LLM hallucinates a non-existent table name as empty string (or omits the field), the agent silently returns client_product schema, so the LLM thinks it has metadata for the made-up table and proceeds.
reproducer: Code path: tools.py:171 `table = (args.get('table') or 'client_product').strip()` — non-empty other table names go to inspect_table; falsy collapses to default with no warning.

description: Validator and prompts allow LLM to declare 'min_rows', 'non_null_columns' freely, but the LLM is not required to call validate_result. If LLM skips validation step and goes straight to final_answer (against SYSTEM_PROMPT rule 5 but not enforced anywhere), an empty/garbage result is reported with confident numbers like 'итого = 0 рублей' for a query that returned zero rows because of a wrong filter.
reproducer: Code path: agent.run has no enforcement that validate_result must precede final_answer; SYSTEM_PROMPT is advisory only.

description: SQL safety regex (_FORBIDDEN, db.py:20) rejects any SELECT whose string-literal or LIKE-pattern contains a forbidden keyword as a substring (DROP/CREATE/EXEC/...). Common legitimate queries like WHERE organization_nm LIKE '%CREATE%' or WHERE manager_name LIKE '%Drop%' are blocked, so the assistant cannot answer questions about clients/managers whose names contain those substrings.
reproducer: from text2sql.db import assert_safe_select; assert_safe_select("SELECT * FROM client_product WHERE organization_nm LIKE '%CREATE%'") -> SQLSafetyError

description: assert_safe_select forbids any ';' anywhere except trailing, including semicolons inside Oracle string literals. Legitimate filters like manager_name = 'Иванов; И.В.' are rejected as 'multi-statement'.
reproducer: assert_safe_select("SELECT * FROM client_product WHERE manager_name = 'Иванов; И.В.'") -> SQLSafetyError(Multi-statement SQL не поддерживается)
